text:
Searching for papers on neural coupling during music listening... 🔍

Here are **10 recent papers on neural coupling during music listening** from PubMed (2025–2026):

---

## 📚 Neural Coupling & Music Listening

### 1. Functional Connectivity of Auditory, Motor, and Reward Networks at Rest and During Music Listening
- **Authors:** Han KYK, et al.
- **Journal:** *Brain Sciences* (2025)
- **DOI:** [10.3390/brainsci16010015](https://doi.org/10.3390/brainsci16010015)
- **TLDR:** Investigated functional connectivity among auditory, motor, and reward networks during focused vs. background music listening using fMRI (N=39 per sample).

### 2. Nightingale: A Multimodal Approach Combining EEG Signals and Audio Features to Enhance Music Therapy
- **Authors:** Chen Z, Yadav N
- **Journal:** *PLoS One* (2026)
- **DOI:** [10.1371/journal.pone.0341497](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0341497) 🆓
- **TLDR:** Presents a novel multimodal system integrating EEG with audio features to predict emotional responses during music therapy — directly relevant to neural coupling measurement.

### 3. Investigating Hemodynamic Patterns During Beat Processing in Cochlear Implant Users
- **Authors:** O'Connell SR, Jounghani AR, Papadopoulos JM, Bortfeld H, Lee Goldsworthy R
- **Journal:** *Auditory Perception & Cognition* (2025)
- **DOI:** [10.1080/25742442.2025.2510182](https://doi.org/10.1080/25742442.2025.2510182) 🆓
- **TLDR:** Uses fNIRS to examine neural beat processing in CI users during music, showing hemodynamic coupling patterns differ from normal-hearing controls.

### 4. Beta- and Gamma-Band Neuromagnetic Oscillations in Stroke Rehab Using Music-Supported Therapy
- **Authors:** Fujioka T, Chen JL, Black SE, Chen JJ, Honjo K, Dawson DR, Ross B
- **Journal:** *Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences* (2025)
- **DOI:** [10.1111/nyas.70041](https://doi.org/10.1111/nyas.70041)
- **TLDR:** Music-supported therapy modulates beta/gamma oscillatory coupling in stroke patients differently from manual training, with contralesional compensatory patterns.

### 5. Listening to Development: How EEG Informs Infant Language and Music Research
- **Authors:** Bradley H, Vanden Bosch der Nederlanden CM, Cirelli LK
- **Journal:** *Infant Behavior and Development* (2025)
- **DOI:** [10.1016/j.infbeh.2025.102125](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.infbeh.2025.102125) 🆓
- **TLDR:** Review of EEG advances in infant auditory perception research, integrating language and music neural entrainment findings. Relevant to Cu's infant hearing work!

### 6. Common Brain Representations of Action and Perception in Newly Learned Melodies
- **Authors:** Chang YF, Ullén F, de Manzano Ö
- **Journal:** *Scientific Reports* (2025)
- **DOI:** [10.1038/s41598-025-00208-x](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-025-00208-x) 🆓
- **TLDR:** fMRI cross-modal classification shows shared neural representations for playing and listening to melodies, demonstrating action-perception coupling in musicians.

### 7. Sensorimotor Synchronization During Finger-Tapping in Cerebellar Ataxia
- **Authors:** Moumdjian L, Feys P, Moens B, Manto M, Cabaraux P, et al.
- **Journal:** *Cortex* (2025)
- **DOI:** [10.1016/j.cortex.2025.04.005](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cortex.2025.04.005)
- **TLDR:** Examines how tempo deviations affect sensorimotor synchronization in cerebellar ataxia patients, probing the cerebellum's role in auditory-motor coupling.

### 8. Alpha-Band Brain Activity Shapes Online Perceptual Learning of Concurrent Speech in Musicians vs. Nonmusicians
- **Authors:** MacLean J, Stirn J, Bidelman GM
- **Journal:** *European Journal of Neuroscience* (2025)
- **DOI:** [10.1111/ejn.70100](https://doi.org/10.1111/ejn.70100)
- **TLDR:** Musical training shapes brain-to-acoustic synchronization (neural entrainment) and alpha oscillatory interactions during concurrent speech perception.

### 9. From Lab to Concert Hall: Effects of Live Performance on Neural Entrainment and Engagement
- **Authors:** Asthagiri A, Loui P
- **Journal:** *bioRxiv* (2025, preprint)
- **DOI:** [10.1101/2025.04.03.646931](https://doi.org/10.1101/2025.04.03.646931) 🆓
- **TLDR:** Compares neural entrainment during live vs. recorded music performance — live music enhances neural coupling and engagement. Super relevant to real-world music neuroscience!

### 10. Real-Time Control of a Hearing Instrument with EEG-Based Attention Decoding
- **Authors:** Hjortkjær J, Wong DDE, Catania A, et al.
- **Journal:** *Journal of Neural Engineering* (2025)
- **DOI:** [10.1088/1741-2552/ad867c](https://doi.org/10.1088/1741-2552/ad867c)
- **TLDR:** Demonstrates real-time EEG attention decoding to control hearing aids, using neural coupling to determine which speech stream a listener is attending to.

---

**104 total results** on PubMed for this query. Papers #5 and #9 seem especially relevant to your research on speaker-listener neural coupling and music/speech processing. Want me to dig deeper into any of these? 🦞
